I can’t say enough good things about Mike Daily. When I started looking for an Arkansas lawyer, I wanted someone who was sharp, professional, and a genuinely good communicator. Someone who could really understand the nuances of what I was dealing with, not just the surface-level details. From day one, Mike exceeded every expectation.

He’s calm, confident, and strategic in a way that instantly makes you feel like you’re in good hands. He’s the kind of lawyer who doesn’t just answer your questions, he helps you see the whole picture and make smart decisions for the long term. I always felt like I had someone steady and thoughtful in my corner.

Mike is also a litigator, which gave me so much confidence. Even though I didn't need that, knowing that if it ever did he would be the one standing next to me made all the difference. He’s the person you want in your strategy room, calm under pressure, quick on his feet, and completely dialed in to the details.

He’s local to Northwest Arkansas and it shows. He knows the people, the courts, and the law inside and out. There’s something really reassuring about working with someone who not only understands the legal side but also the community you’re a part of.

I always told myself that when it was all said and done, I would write this review. And that’s partly true, but now I know I’ll be using Mike Daily for any legal needs that come up in the future. If you’re looking for a trusted Arkansas attorney who’s professional, kind, and truly exceptional at what he does, he’s the one.

Might be great if you can get an appointment? I’ll never know. I called and submitted an online request a few months ago. No answer, no response. Called again recently and setup an appointment for a phone appointment as I’m out of state. Never got the follow up email that was mentioned but whatever. Appointment time came and went, both their time zone and mine, no call. Things happen so it’s fine, called and made another appointment for a few hours later same day - no call at that appointment time. I’m busy working and have given up, when I get an apology email and a proposal for another time even later in the day. I don’t see the email because I’m busy and then get a missed call while I’m in a meeting. Sigh. So last attempt, cause I understand things come up - I reply and make an appointment for a new day later in the week. No call lol. It’s kind of comical. 4 attempted appointments and no call except the one appointment I never responded to? Not sure I would advise to with a place that can’t manage an appointment, or where the owner is completely unreachable, so I’ll look elsewhere. Just say you aren’t taking new clients or don’t do phone appointments if it’s that overwhelming.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Michelle,
This is Jamison Bonds, the owner of the firm.
It looks like I personally dropped the ball on you… more than once.
No excuses. That’s embarrassing and I don’t blame you for feeling frustrated and wanting to pull your hair out. I’d feel the same way.
This is not how we normally do business – just look at the mountain of 5-star reviews we have built over the years. We aim to give every person we touch a wonderful experience.
That’s why we want to offer to go above and beyond and make this up to you in one of two ways.
1. We have a phone consultation.
We’ll waive our $150 consultation fee.
Instead, WE WILL PAY YOU $150 for any frustration we might have caused.
Additionally, I’ll provide you with my cell phone number: (501) 472-0460.
2 – We’ll refer you to the Best Estate Lawyers we know
After this, I don’t blame you if you don’t want to risk wasting any more time dealing with us.
If that’s the case, we don’t want you to have to keep digging to find top-tier lawyers that work in estate planning.

Here’s a list of the best estate lawyers around the state that’ll give you a wonderful experience and answer all your questions:

Fort Smith:
Drew Harmon 479-785-0123
Dennis Sbannatto 479-785-3756

Conway:
Chris Rippy – 501-428-9139
Frank Dudeck – 501-327-3527

Greater Little Rock:
Justin Elrod – 501-847-1311

NWA:
Todd Whatley – 479-601-4119


Feel free to reach out anytime, and you’ll be surprised at how well you're treated. We trust and believe that the value we can provide you will be worth 10 times what you put in.

Warm regards,
Jamison Bonds

If you're looking for an attorney who will genuinely fight for your rights and make you feel like a priority, look no further than Joey McCutchen. From the very beginning, Joey demonstrated a level of diligence and expertise that is truly exceptional. He approached my case not just with professional skill, but with a deep commitment to justice and what was right. His knowledge of the law and his strategic acumen were evident at every turn, and his unwavering dedication made all the difference. Joey doesn't just handle cases; he advocates passionately for his clients. I can't recommend him highly enough!

Intuitive and caring... Mr. McCutchen was everything his well-earned reputation had suggested before I retained him. He was responsive and definitely did everything in his power to make me feel important and listened to. It was a wonderful experience. Both he and the staff that I dealt with were truly professional. I do not hesitate in fully recommending Mr McCutchen for your legal needs!
Joey is the best lawyer nothing but the best experience ever !!

Joey McCutchen is exceptionally professional, helpful, intelligent, and efficient. My father was severely injured in an accident when a car ran a red light while he was crossing an intersection, leading to major surgery on his spine. When I reached out to Joey's firm, he immediately took on my father's case. Remarkably, he quickly located a surveillance video of the incident that even the local police couldn't find! This video became the crucial piece of evidence in our case. Joey then swiftly secured the maximum settlement from the driver's insurance company.

My entire family is deeply grateful for Joey's expertise, professionalism, and genuine care. Without him and his McCutchen Napurano Law Firm, we can't imagine how we would have won this case. I highly recommend Joey and his law firm to anyone in a similar situation. Joey will fight to get you the justice you deserve. He truly is the best!

When it comes to defending the public's right to information, Joey McCutchen stands out as an exceptional advocate and legal powerhouse. His  unwavering commitment to freedom of information and transparency is truly commendable and sets him apart in the legal field.

From the moment you engage with their team, it is clear that the McCutchen Napurano Law Firm is deeply passionate about upholding the principles of openness and accountability. Their expertise in navigating the complex landscape of information rights and public access laws is second to none. Whether it's handling high-profile cases or guiding clients through intricate legal procedures, their knowledge and skill are evident in every aspect of their work. Their dedication goes beyond just legal representation; they are genuine advocates for the cause, working tirelessly to ensure that information remains accessible and that transparency prevails.

The firm's impressive track record of success speaks volumes about their proficiency and dedication. They have consistently achieved favorable outcomes in cases involving information rights, setting important precedents and making significant contributions to the broader fight for transparency.

In addition to their professional prowess, Joey McCutchen and Stephen Napurano are approachable, responsive, and supportive. They keep clients informed at every step of the process, providing clarity and reassurance in what can often be a complex and daunting legal journey.

If you are seeking a law firm that is not only a formidable legal advocate but also a staunch supporter of the fundamental right to information, look no further. Their exceptional service, combined with their passion for transparency and accountability, makes them a standout choice for anyone in need of expert representation in the realm of freedom of information.

I discovered the Cabot School Board was violating State open meeting laws in the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act by conducting illegal meetings. Problem was that I could not find any avenue of accountability until I meet Joey McCutchen. Joey took the case, and we filed suit against the Board. With an unlimited supply of taxpayer funds the Board, through their high-priced attorneys, threw up every legal hurdle they could but Joey busted through them all and when it was all said and done, he forced them to admit they violated the law on 17 counts, and we got a signed order from the Judge. When you need legal services finding someone with brains and expertise are important. However, finding an attorney who is also full of Passion...Resolve...and Stamina is rare.....That attorney is Joey McCutchen.

I cannot say enough good things about the McCutchen Napurano Law Firm. Joey and Stephen are not just incredible attorneys, they are great human beings who truly care about people. They have a calling to help people in need and have a proven track record of success!
Top priority in my case this firm fights for the little guy...I lost it all but with his help I'm gonna get it all back, praise the good Lord for joey

I can not say enough good things about my attorney, Mr Napurano. My mother in law and I were in a very bad car wreck that left us both with injuries and me with a total car.  I suffered neck and back injuries. My elderly mother in law who had previously suffered a stroke had facial injuries and ptsd which left her scared  to get in a car for months. When we found out the at fault driver was severely under insured, we knew we would need an attorney. I called McCutchen and Napurano. Best decision I could've made. Mr Napurano took all my anxiety and worries over medical bills off my shoulders so I could focus on getting well. He was very realistic regarding our outcome and promised nothing he knew he could not do for us. He guaranteed a flat fee with no hidden cost.  Just knowing we had someone looking out for us side was such a relief. Whenever we had questions? He  answered our or returned all calls promptly.  I always felt he was on top of my case. We only wanted what was fair, and when the insurance balked at our proposal, he refused to back down. He was courteous, showed genuine interest, and always made me feel like I mattered. Great communicator, answered all of my questions when he could, and if he wasn't sure, he would find the answer and call me back. I would have NEVER made it thru this had it not been for him. I feel like it was a true blessing that he took me and my mother in law on for clients. I could never thank him enough for what he did for us.

Joey McCutchen is a great client advocate. After a motor vehicle accident, he and his team walked us through the process to aquire appropriate and necessary medical treatments, and he negotiated for us a very good settlement. I recommend his law firm for anyone needing such assistance.
Very professional, made the experience a lot less stressful and took care of everything while keeping me informed.

Last Tuesday, September 26th, I called and reached out to my former Southside High School classmate, Joey McCutchen, for some legal advice. My mom and family were in a situation with an individual that had been going on for two months with no resolve.
I remembered Joey McCutchen to be an outstanding student, athlete and most importantly, individual, from our high school days.  I live in San Antonio and felt helpless not being able to help my mom and family from afar.  I called Joey McCutchen and spoke to him at length about the situation we were dealing with, getting emotional at times.  Joey listened patiently and compassionately as I told him the details and asked him for help.  By the end of the conversation, Joey had given me specific instructions to follow and told me to keep in touch daily.  I immediately did as Joey advised and by Friday morning the situation had be completely resolved beyond our expectations.

I can not express how thankful my family is to Joey McCutchen for the legal advice he gave me and my family last week.  Without Joey stepping in to help, I feel the stressful situation would be ongoing today.

Attorney Joey McCutchen is an outstanding, knowledgeable lawyer who will fight for his clients with patience, compassion and tenacity to insure a prompt and fair outcome is reached.  My family and I highly recommend Attorney Joey McCutchen as a lawyer and upstanding individual. I've worked hard all my life. Made money, saved money, and put money aside. Even so there were still times that I needed help along the way. This morning while counting my gratefuls. Joey McCutchens crossed my mind. Years ago' I was in a situation and needed a lawyer. I was physically broken, mentally exhausted, spiritually drained and almost financially broke. I was selling everything that I had and depleting my savings just to have a little cash flow. Trying to keep up with medical bills and day to day living expenses.  I needed help and i prayed constantly. Then i hired Joey and his law firm. For me it was a God thing to hire him! Now because of he and his law firm. I can still live a life! I deal with physical issues but my mind is free, I still remember the worry, the frustration and hurt of the whole  experience.and I learned that when your mind is exhausted your whole body is exhausted. Worry can kill a broken man! and I was broken in every way. I was running outta money and couldnt afford to pay my doctors. Joey immediately made sure that I continued to get the best medical care available. He and his staff would take time to talk about all my concerns and worries. Yall listen! I prayed, I hired Joey" and having nothing to do with money! God did miracles for this broken man during that time. I got my mind back and found my good heart again. I could focus on getting better and staying healthy because God sent me hope. Our paths seldome cross but today he and his goodness crossed my heart and mind. While thanking God for my gratefuls.I realized that without Joey McCutchens and his good folks. I would have much less to thank God for. Thank you for everything God and Thank you Joey for your goodness and for being good at what you do. I agree with Jesus that there is none good but God. And if you find a good man then you have found a Godly man.
Joey and his team is the best! They work hard for you and is always available if you have questions or concerns. They go above and beyond to serve you!

reviews.text: Left from there in tears today.  I had a very unique situation and needed a letter drafted for one of my children. She had already been retained and all fees paid.  I presented at her office this afternoon after leaving 2 messages that had yet been returned.  After speaking to her receptionist and being informed she was with a client and had zoom court at 3pm, I called my child and their agent and was asked by them to wait to see her as our request would only take a few minutes and was urgent. An enormous amount of money was to be received by my child  upon receipt of this letter.  The receptionist told me, Carrie would not be seeing me today  at all and was aware of my 2 previous messages and the urgency of my request.  I asked will the letter at least be drafted today? I was told, she couldn’t guarantee  that either. I went to my car crying out of pure disappointment.  We retained another attorney and got our request taken care of within a hour. They called my child, as they are in another state that’s why I’m handling their in person business and all of a sudden had time to meet with us at 4:30pm.  We declined as our new attorney was already meeting our needs and had drafted and faxed the letter. On what started off as a very happy day, with exiting news for our child was abruptly dismissed.  Last, she was recommended to me by a friend of mine whom is an attorney, but doesn’t practice in our county.  I called my friend, she was devastated at how I/we were treated and apologized immensely for the recommendation.  Final words, disappointed, frustrated, and hurt.
Carrie Handled a wreckless driving ticket for me went to trail and won

I have sent an email to address my concerns. However, the secretary at the time of my calling was rude, had no answers, and did not ask for adequate information to assess whether or not she could assist me. If this can be fixed, I will update my review. Carrie’s reputation is astounding and essential, but this was glaring and unacceptable.

I called to inquire about services and spoke with the receptionist. From the beginning, her tone was negative and unwelcoming. I tried to explain my situation, but the first thing she mentioned was that it would cost $2,500. When I asked if I could first speak with the attorney before committing, she told me the lawyer does not give free legal advice and that payment must be made upfront. While I completely understand that attorneys charge for their time, the way it was communicated felt dismissive and purely transactional.

Because of the receptionist’s attitude and energy, I decided not to move forward. I can’t speak to the quality of the attorneys themselves, but this initial encounter left me with a very poor impression. When I politely declined, the receptionist hung up on me. The overall interaction gave the impression that the priority was collecting a check, rather than genuinely understanding a potential client’s needs. It felt like no guidance or information would be offered unless money was presented first, which came across as disingenuous.

I cannot thank Bailey Riggs enough for the exceptional representation and guidance he provided. Throughout my case, which required multiple court dates due to scheduling changes and no-shows, Bailey remained steadfast and dedicated. reviews.ownerResponse:
Nathan,
I am surprised and disappointed by your review. You had multiple prior felonies. Your original offer was substantial pen time. I was in court to announce the matter was for jury trial but you did not appear despite being notified by both the court and me so a failure to appear was issued. It was your choice to take a plea instead of waiting in jail for trial. You could have had a petition to revoke a previous suspended sentence. My efforts avoided a penitentiary sentence, got you out of jail from your failure to appear and avoided a revocation of the current felony suspended sentence you are serving. The forfeiture to which you refer was simply an agreement that you did not claim the monies seized from illegal activities. You are out today to write this review solely because I was looking out for you.

In the end, he managed to get all my felony charges dropped.

There are quite a few attorneys in Fort Smith. What distinguishes Mr. Honeycutt from the rest is his remarkable ability to connect with and understand the people he works with (prosecuting attorneys and judges) very well and effortlessly grasping what motivates them. This requires knowledge, experience, and rare wisdom that you don't find with most attorneys.

My employment involves constant security background monitoring from the Texas state government. If the charges hadn't been dropped, I would have been terminated by my employer for sure. My wife was also going through open-heart surgery at the same time. If the outcome had been any different, we would have lost our medical insurance, and the surgery process would have been incomplete.

I hired Marvin to defend me in court against the unjustified charges, and he went above and beyond that. In the end, he saved my family.

reviews.text: Mrs. Roni was absolutely amazing. My wife and I had been fighting for our daughter since she was 3 years old. Her father was physically and mentally abusive and she begged to not go back. We were told by many other attorneys that the protective order was a waste of time, and it would never work. After searching for weeks, we found Roni a week prior to our hearing and she was able to take the case. He had joint custody at this time, and our daughter was scared to death to even see him in public. The protective order was granted and within a month, he dropped insurance, stopped paying medical bills, and had an open criminal case. Without hesitation he signed a waiver to get rid of his parental rights and allow me to adopt her. Our daughter is 7 years old now and has not ever been so happy. She has a long journey on recovering, but is doing an amazing job. Can never thank Mrs. Roni enough for the hard work and dedication she put into this case. Thank you so much! After 4 years in court, it is finally over.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks for the detailed review and trusting our firm to handle your cases. It's our pleasure to help you - such a deserving family and we are thrilled you had the outcome you did!

reviews.text:
I was going through a dispute to terminate a temporary guardianship that I had with my sister.  Subsequently, this led into a custody battle between my son’s mother and I.  I found myself fighting for my son against a coalition of two female aggressors.  I originally retained representation through two separate law firms over the course of a year but had to leave both because they were completely ineffective in showing proper attention to my case.
I was at my whit’s end and out of money when I found Roni nearly a year after filing the original termination of guardianship.
Roni was the consummate professional.  She did not sell me a bill of goods or tell me what I wanted to hear just to get my business but was straight to the point about the fight we had ahead of us.  When there was mis-communication between opposing counsels and the court, she was on top of every detail, taking leadership and even making allowances for myself on the financial end so I would not absorb all of the costs.  Her retainer and services were extremely reasonable and competitive.
In court, Roni was prepared amidst an onslaught of attacks from two separate fronts.  She was calm, cool, collected, yet aggressive when the moment warranted.  She owned the room through poise, preparation, and confidence.  I truly believe finding her when I did was a form of Divine intervention in my son’s life.  He is in a safer place right now with a bright future because of her.
We won our case, and I have Roni Bryant to thank for it.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Ryan for this thoughtful review and for trusting us with such an important matter! We are so happy for you and the outcome of your case!

reviews.text: I had a legal problem and being new to Fort Smith AI, I hired roni. I could not be more pleased. She is a real professional as well as a person with compassion. I recommend her for your neeeds.

reviews.text: I have given Trae Bryant a very hard time with my case. It hasn’t been the easiest for him and I actually ended up letting him go as my attorney. If people would just shut up and listen to their attorneys advice (especially myself) things would go more so in your favor. Trae took my case again when things went south with the other party, I had lost hope in all honesty, but he snatched my case back up and was on it by 7 am the next morning. Within the little amount of time I’ve had Trae back representing me it has been nothing but a blessing. I know my son is safe and I know Trae really does have good intentions and has my back. He is 100% for anyone who is in a custody battle, whether it be a nasty case or an easy case. You just have to take his advice very seriously, he will not let you down. The only person that can let you down is yourself. So thank you Trae for waking me up and making me grow up. I have grown into the best mom I can be for all of my children. Most importantly thank you for having my back even when I wasn’t right and was blinded by “love”. He’s an amazing attorney and worth the money. Always responds and has things in order ready to go on the day of court. Thank you, Trae! You’re the best.

There were blatant inconsistencies in the witness's stories, and Whitfield sat silent. Then had the nerve at the end of it all to offer to appeal, but would cost 3x as much because "it would be harder". Well the 1st time around was apparently too hard for him, why would I trust him on something "harder". If you have a slam dunk case, I'm sure Whitfield can handle it. If it requires actual defense, I'd look elsewhere. I bet he does a good job defending himself against this review, but the truth is the truth. I can’t speak to the quality of the attorneys themselves, but this initial encounter left me with a very poor impression. When I politely declined, the receptionist hung up on me. reviews.text: terrible experience.  When I called to set up appointment I explained that it was for my 95 yr old mother and sister who live out of state and that this was the only day available and what we wanted.  Appointment was at 1:30 and at 11:30 I received a message confirming the appointment.  when we arrived I was told the appointment was canceled because Mr Moore decided he did not want to talk with us. I lost a day off work and will now have to wait until next year when my sister is back in town. Hopefully my mother will still be here!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Eric, I am happy to see you have admitted to having received the cancellation notice, as well as the two voicemails that you received in an attempt to speak with you hours prior to 1:30. To reiterate again, Mr. Moore felt the services you were requesting would be handled best with an attorney who specializes in that specific practice of Law. The possibility of this happening was also warned to you on the initial phone call; and you still insisted on scheduling the consultation with our office. The attorney you were being referred to was made aware of your case, needs and the status of your appointment hours before the scheduled consult at 1:30. This information was also provided to you via voicemail. However, you appeared in our office in an aggressive, rude, intolerable and violent manner stating that you were not aware the appointment had been cancelled. Even after an explanation was given to you a second time you began yelling, cussing, pacing around our office in a very aggressive demeanor.  You also had to be told to not point your finger in my face, as well as to not call me derogatory names multiple times and speak in an acceptable tone and volume. Truthfully I am surprised to not have a broken piece office decor or a hole made in our wall after witnessing your unpredictable and unstable presentation at the time. After having to tell you to leave or we would be calling the police on you and your erratic behavior, your mother and sister also came in to yell and create an unnecessary scene. As a 28 year old woman who a grown man was trying to intimidate, I will make no apologies for how I protect myself within the work place when presented with such situations. I send my best wishes to your mom and hope you receive the legal aid you are searching for.

reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
I had the privilege of being represented by Cory Bridges, and I can confidently say he is one of the best in the field. From the start, Cory handled my case with precision and professionalism. He was always quick to respond, clear in his communication, and incredibly kind throughout the entire process. What really stood out to me was the respect he commands in the courtroom—it's clear that even the judges and court staff hold him in high regard. reviews.text: I’m giving it a 3 star at this point. I applied first of February and was approved. I was told that attorney would be contacting me that week. I had just been made homeless. It was end of April before my assigned attorney finally got back to me. He sent paperwork which I promptly filled out and returned. It’s June 14th and my soon to be ex informs me that he heard my attorney had been arrested. I called and spoke with Kim. She is why I’m giving stars at all. Turns out they assigned me a new attorney who sent my papers to the safehouse address in another state. I never received papers because my first assigned attorney told me I had to move back to Arkansas. I emailed him when I moved back to Arkansas and gave him the address I’m staying at… when Kim shared this I broke down crying…Kim did not slack on her job. She went above and beyond to comforted me and reassured me she would help. Me straighten this out.

I came to hickey and hull in one of the worst times of my life . I was appointed the attorney Randy shock, I was dealing with a lot of false allegations from DHS, not only did Randy drive 3 and 1/2 hours to my court case but he answered all of my emails and calmed my concerns, taking all the supportive information I had to offer for my case Randy and his team were amazing and really saw me through a heap of a mess, I can only hope to keep Randy on as my attorney for future needs, you won't find better. Shalom
Savanna S

Kevin represented me in both a criminal fraud matter and my divorce simultaneously, and his work was outstanding. These were high-stress, high-stakes situations, and he handled them with professionalism, precision, and confidence.

He is direct and honest about expectations, communicates clearly, and makes sure you understand your options at every stage. I never felt lost or ignored. Kevin stayed calm and strategic even when emotions and pressure were high, which made all the difference.

Having one attorney successfully manage both a criminal case and a divorce at the same time speaks volumes about his skill and dedication. If you need an attorney who is intelligent, responsive, and truly capable of handling complex situations, I would recommend Kevin without hesitation.
Randy Shock helped me win my case and get my kids back from a horrible situation!! He's a wonderful lawyer and if I ever have to go to court again I will definitely use him again!!!
Brad Hull is the best lawyer in town!! Thank you so much for everything!!

My case went very well. Mr. Shock did a wonderful job representing me in the court room. Our hearing was over in 15 minutes, and I never had to speak a word. The court orders took a little long to come back for me. They did a good job and I would use them again.

I felt he really didn't offer much for us in family court. Yes he showed up but he didn't do much of anything for fighting for our case. We went with another attorney far more aggressive and that attorney did clean up the mess and win!

My personal factual experience is not defamatory... fact is we weren't impressed and my honest opinion and facts on the matter is allowed to be shared. There is nothing made up or lies here. That being said as an attorney you should know that. Wish you the best of luck do better for your clients!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Charles:
This review is irrelevant and we haven’t been your lawyer of record for over 12 years. We ask that you remove this defamatory review.

reviews.text:
If you’re looking to spend a lot of money for minimal results, this is the place to go.

- Lack of confidence in law: Poorly represented in key moments.

- Lack of consistency: Promises made were not delivered.

- Lack of accountability: No ownership of mistakes or missteps.

- Lack of professionalism: Prioritized convenience over advocacy.

- Excessive charges: Even basic updates were billed, despite being owed updates.

- Unreachable in urgent situations: Critical moments lacked support.

Kevin Hickey himself was representing me, I expected a strong advocate, but he focused on what was quickest and easiest for him. I was told one thing would happen, only to face an entirely different reality in court.
For $8,000, I’ve never felt more misrepresented.

I can’t help but wonder if Kevin truly believes this was his best effort.

Would 100% recommend ! Our case was a pretty hard one with my husband trying to adopt my daughter and her biological father being prideful and fighting it and lying even though his actions have always showed my daughter was the least of his priorities. Brad Hull (and his Para,  Michele) did amazing representing us and was also very open and honest with it being a 50/50 chance on what the outcome would be as well as explaining how expensive it might get or cheapest option with the options of our next steps .Needless to say He helped us kick butt and bring the truth and facts to light to protect my daughter from her bio dad . Kevin Hickey Law will continue to be our first option when we need a lawyer.
Let's  just say, don't  feel my money was  well spent. And probably won't use them again
Kind
Awesome people would recommend

reviews.text: I obtained a consult with Mr. Hickey.  He was pleasant and his legal aide was as well.  I had sent documents for their review and never had a response. reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Smith, our initial consult fee is for the initial meeting and to discuss strategies involving your legal issue, plans for going forward, and answer any of your questions.  To go beyond that requires additional fees for the additional work.  Many times the client does not have all of the paperwork we need to review at the initial consultation.  We will then generally ask the client to gather that paperwork and provide it to us.  We tried to get the necessary documents from you so that we could quote you an appropriate fee but did not receive them.  We most certainly care about our clients, both clients that have hired us and prospective clients such as yourself.  We would be happy to sit down with you again for no charge/fee and discuss options for going forward if you are still interested in our firm.  Just let us know.

We advised you that in order to modify an order, you have to prove a material and substantial change in the circumstances has occurred after the previous order had been entered. We advised you that we did not think you had enough to modify the previous order. Therefore, we did not want to charge you a fee when more than likely you would not be successful. We want to do what is best for our clients and are not just looking for a fee.

((UPDATE))

5 years ago, I received standard visitation after fighting for full custody.

Let's break this down...
I've had plenty of time to think about this one...

**Every Other Weekend**:
- Friday 5pm to Sunday 5pm = 48 hours every other weekend.
- In a month, this typically happens twice, so: 2 x 48 hours = 96 hours.
**Every Wednesday**:
- 5pm to 9pm = 4 hours.
- In a typical month, there are about 4 Wednesdays, so: 4 x 4 hours = 16 hours.

Now, let's sum these:

96 hours (from the weekends) + 16 hours (from the Wednesdays) = 112 hours per month.

Therefore, with the provided schedule, a father would see their kids for approximately 112 hours each month.

To determine how many days the 112 hours equate to, divide the total hours by 24 (since there are 24 hours in a day, right?).

112÷24= 4.67 days...

So, 112 hours equates to approximately 4.67 days in a month.

SO EQUATING TO 4.67 DAYS A MONTH or in full summary 280.2 days to be exact for the last 5 years that I've been permitted to see my child because of this """standard visitation""" typically given to fathers.

So, to any father or mother who loves their child/children and has had to endure this kidnapping, know that you're not alone.

I assure you that each day has been remembered.

This was written to inform those who don't understand the robbery that occurs with this "standard visitation."
Take the time and do the math.

THIS IS CRUEL AND UNFAIR PUNISHMENT.

In 2016, I was injured on the job while working as a RN for Mercy Hospital. Rather than helping me recover from the injuries, Mercy chose to deny my worker’s compensation claim and fire me. To begin with, I hired an attorney other than Eddie Walker. This other attorney had my claim for nearly three years and got nowhere with it, not even to a hearing. I finally asked the other attorney to withdraw from my case and took everything to Mr. Walker.
By the time I consulted with Mr. Walker, there were literally thousands and thousands of pages of medical records, depositions, and other documents for him to read through to get the full picture of my case. Even though my injuries happened years prior, and even though there was a mountain of paperwork for Mr. Walker to dig through, he still had us in court within a few short months of accepting my case. To date, we have been to court four times for this worker’s compensation claim, and we have won every time. Mr. Walker always showed up to court completely prepared, with an absolute understanding of everything about my case and full comprehension of the laws that govern it. He has also written several briefs to the Courts regarding this claim and every single one of them have been a masterpiece. The briefs have also shown complete preparedness, an absolute understanding of my case, and a deep grasp of the law. In addition, Mr. Walker has a phenomenal office staff. They all work as a team, ensuring their clients are well taken care of, calls and emails are quickly answered, and everything is running smoothly.
Mercy has fought this worker’s compensation claim for nine and a half long years. They have played unfairly by paying doctors that never met me, never even examined me, to write letters saying I was not injured. They have appealed nearly every ruling to the Full Commission and this case now sets at the Arkansas Court of Appeals. Yet, Mr. Walker has stood by me every step of the way since taking on my case. He has proven he is not afraid to take on challenging cases. He has more than put in the time and effort necessary to fight for me and I am grateful for all he has done.  To me, Eddie Walker is a mighty warrior fighting for justice.

If you are in need of a great attorney then look no further that Eddie Walker for all of your workers comp. Issues they have great customer service and take the time to get to know you and address all of your concerns. I would never choose any other lawyer than Eddie Walker for my workers comp problems!!!!!!
Helped me with a workmans comp case with my previous employer. We won the case. Eddie and his team were amazing through the entire process. I highly recommend

Mr. Walker is the best there is. Knows what he is talking about and helps you every step of the way. Just finished a multiple year long case and I never could of gotten what I did if I hadn't of went to them. Thank you, Mr. Walker.
